# [Clang] Assertion `Unqualified ? hasSameUnqualifiedType(X, Y) : hasSameType(X, Y)' failed.

**Author:** yijan4845
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/111300
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** clang:frontend, confirmed, crash-on-valid, regression:16, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2026-03-01T17:11:38Z

## Body

Compiler Explorer: [https://godbolt.org/z/dbjjexs4f](https://godbolt.org/z/dbjjexs4f)

Attempting to compile the following program with Clang Assertion Trunk:
```c
void foo(const void *pcv, void *pvv, int i) {
    1 ? *pcv : *pvv;
}
```

Stack dump:
```
clang: /root/llvm-project/clang/lib/AST/ASTContext.cpp:13930: clang::QualType clang::ASTContext::getCommonSugaredType(clang::QualType, clang::QualType, bool): Assertion `Unqualified ? hasSameUnqualifiedType(X, Y) : hasSameType(X, Y)' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
```

---

### Comment 2 - shafik

Looks like a clang-16 regression: https://godbolt.org/z/q4E99EfWG

---

### Comment 3 - shafik

CC @mizvekov git bisect points to you commit here: 67e22983115451ef5512ad2813dd337762c52da3

---

### Comment 4 - zyn0217

@mizvekov
Looking at
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/64779455b8f4875c7de690dd4c3e324dbbcb3029/clang/lib/Sema/SemaExprCXX.cpp#L7094-L7095

I think this should be `return Context.getCommonSugaredType(LTy, RTy, true)` because we cannot have qualifiers on `void`?

---

### Comment 5 - mizvekov

Yes we can have qualifiers on void, they are different types: https://godbolt.org/z/3nb55735K

But this should have `/*Unqualified=*/true` in any case, because we are looking at the types of parameters in function types, and in those, top level qualifiers are just sugar.

---

### Comment 6 - mizvekov

Actually never mind, I looked at the example too fast, this is dereferencing the pointers within a function declaration.

There is something else going on wrong here, the types are still different, so I don't think we should have gotten to this point.

---
